rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 17, 2011, Line 10, PLAINTIFF CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, Motion To Transfer And Consolidate. Denied. rulings.Case Title: MERYL MIKAL et al VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 17, 2011, Line 14, DEFENDANT SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the third cause of action, overruled as to the fourth and fifth cause of action, and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. As to the third cause of action, plaintiffs need to be plead with more particularity the elements of intent and causation (i.e. what they would have done differently had they had known the allegedly concealed facts). The fourth and fifth causes of action are adequately pled. Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit of the sixth cause of action. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. ELITE CONCORD GROUP LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attached Order And Writ Of Attachment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 17, 2011, Line 10. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attached Order And Writ Of Attachment. Denied. Plaintiff did not file a proof of service of the summons and complaint on the defendant against whom it seeks an attachment as required by CCP 484.040(a) and that defendant denies that it was served. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, November 17, 2011, Line 20, DEFENDANTS STEVEN KREMER, SANDRA KREMER, KATALINA DRESNER Motion To Strike Pltfs Complaint In Its Entirety. Off calendar due to the ruling on the demurrer. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. DEFENDANTS STEVEN KREMER, SANDRA KREMER, KATALINA DRESNER Demurrer to complaint. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege claims that are not time-barred and that were not and could not have part of the prior lawsuits. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
